name: Expense Tracker
description: Property expense management with category breakdown, receipt upload, recurring expenses, and budget tracking.

list.description: Filterable expense list view with category summary bar, data table, and date range controls

create.description: New expense entry form with category select, amount input, description, and receipt upload

summary.description: Category summary widget with pie chart breakdown and per-category running totals

visual_brief: Expense management interface with a header row containing title, date range selector, and an 'Add Expense' button. A category summary bar below shows colored pills for each expense category (Maintenance, Insurance, Utilities, etc.) with running totals. The main area is a data table with columns: date, description, category badge, property, amount, receipt icon, and status. Filter controls sit above the table for category and property filtering. The create preset shows a form with category select, amount input, description, property picker, and receipt upload. Summary preset displays a pie chart of category breakdown.

responsive.mobile: Table becomes a card list — each expense as a stacked card. Category summary pills wrap. Add button is a FAB. Filters collapse into a filter drawer.
responsive.tablet: Table layout maintained with horizontal scroll for extra columns. Category bar fits in one row.
responsive.desktop: Full table with all columns visible. Category summary bar spans the top. Comfortable spacing.

layout_hints.ledger_priority: Lead with expense totals and category scanability, then the underlying records. Users should quickly understand where money is going before they inspect individual receipts.
layout_hints.form_transition: Create and edit flows should feel like a continuation of the ledger, not a different application. Preserve category and property context around the form when possible.
layout_hints.summary_role: The summary chart or category pills should support the table rather than compete with it as a separate analytics dashboard.

motion.micro: Category filters and add-expense actions may sharpen slightly on hover. Status or receipt states may brighten enough to confirm selection or upload completion.
motion.transitions: Date-range and category filtering should preserve confidence in the ledger by updating smoothly without reordering the whole experience unexpectedly.

keyboard: Header actions, filters, category summaries, and expense records should remain reachable in a logical order.
announcements: Expense amount, category, property, and receipt state should be exposed semantically so the ledger remains interpretable without color badges or table styling alone.

composition.ExpenseTracker: Container(d-section, flex-col, gap-4) > [Header + Filters + CategorySummary + ExpenseTable]
composition.ExpenseRow: Row(d-data-row) > [Date + Description + CategoryBadge(d-annotation) + Property + Amount(mono-data) + ReceiptIcon? + StatusBadge(d-annotation)]
